task_completion.description: Log in to the Rancher dashboard
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI dashboard is visible in the web browser, indicating successful authentication.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the deployments in the 'ecommerce-core' namespace
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI shows the Workloads or Deployments page, specifically filtered for or displaying the 'ecommerce-core' namespace.
The Rancher UI (either form view or YAML editor) for 'checkout-service' shows the Readiness Probe configured with type 'HTTP GET', path '/health', and port '8080'.
The Rancher UI (either form view or YAML editor) for 'inventory-service' shows the Readiness Probe configured with type 'HTTP GET', path '/health', and port '8080'.
The Deployments or Pods list in Rancher (or kubectl output) shows 'checkout-service' with a green active state and 1/1 pods ready.
The Deployments or Pods list in Rancher (or kubectl output) shows 'inventory-service' with a green active state and 1/1 pods ready.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Rancher web interface or genuine kubectl terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Rancher GUI in a web browser or typing commands in a real terminal, rather than editing a standalone text file.

integrity.description: The 1/1 Ready state is genuinely displayed by the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: The ready state is visible within the standard Rancher UI tables or standard terminal output, without signs of text manipulation or mocked HTML.

integrity.description: The agent manually configured the probe settings rather than pasting a pre-existing complete YAML file from an external source.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent navigates through the Rancher UI to the specific deployment settings or uses 'kubectl edit' to modify the existing YAML directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target namespace: 'ecommerce-core'. Target deployments: 'checkout-service' and 'inventory-service'. Expected readiness probe configuration for both: Type = 'httpGet', Path = '/health', Port = 8080. Success requires both deployments to reach a 1/1 Ready state.
